metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students develop directorial vision, script analysis skills, and basic script writing and development practices. This course emphasizes understanding dramatic literature, blocking and staging, and bringing creative vision to the stage.
metadata.general_description: A course focused on directorial vision, script analysis, script writing, and staging for theatre.

big_ideas.statement: Directors shape the creative vision that brings scripts to life on stage.
big_ideas.elaboration: Directors interpret dramatic literature, make artistic choices about staging and design, and guide actors and creative teams in realizing a unified artistic vision.

big_ideas.statement: Script analysis reveals layers of meaning, character motivation, and thematic content.
big_ideas.elaboration: Close examination of dramatic texts uncovers dramatic structure, character relationships, themes, and subtext that inform directorial choices and artistic interpretation.

big_ideas.statement: Script development involves collaborative creation of original dramatic works.
big_ideas.elaboration: Writers create scripts that engage audiences through character, dialogue, conflict, and resolution, which can originate from lived experience, adaptation, or imagination.

big_ideas.statement: Staging and movement communicate meaning and support dramatic action.
big_ideas.elaboration: Directors use space, movement, and actor positioning to enhance storytelling, clarify dramatic relationships, and create visual interest and dramatic impact.

competencies.raw: Develop directorial vision and interpretive choices
i_can_statements: I can identify a thematic focus or interpretive concept for a script
I can develop a directorial vision that shapes all creative elements
I can make deliberate artistic choices about tone, style, and approach
I can articulate how my directorial choices serve the play's themes and action
I can plan actor movement and positioning that serves the dramatic action
I can use the stage space creatively to communicate meaning and relationships
I can develop blocking that is clear, purposeful, and visually interesting
I can adapt staging to different spaces and performance contexts

competencies.raw: Write original scripts and dramatic material
i_can_statements: I can develop original characters with distinct voices and motivations
I can write realistic or stylized dialogue that reveals character and advances action
I can structure dramatic scenes with clear objectives and dramatic tension
I can write complete short scripts with setting, characters, and stage directions

competencies.raw: Develop scripts through adaptation and devised theatre approaches
i_can_statements: I can adapt literary works or personal narratives for the stage
I can collaboratively develop scripts through improvisation and devised methods
I can create scripts from diverse source materials and inspiration
I can revise and refine scripts based on workshop and rehearsal experience

competencies.raw: Analyze dramatic texts and identify key elements
i_can_statements: I can identify plot structure, character development, and thematic content in scripts
I can analyze dialogue for subtext, character motivation, and dramatic function
I can understand setting and how it contributes to dramatic meaning
I can recognize dramatic techniques and literary devices in plays

competencies.raw: Examine relationships between script, design, and production elements
i_can_statements: I can understand how design elements support and enhance dramatic action
I can analyze how staging choices affect audience perception and meaning
I can recognize how lighting, sound, and set design contribute to storytelling
I can evaluate relationships between directorial vision and production choices

competencies.raw: Understand dramatic structure and narrative techniques
i_can_statements: I can identify dramatic structure elements such as exposition, rising action, and climax
I can understand how scenes function within overall dramatic structure
I can analyze character arcs and how characters change through the play
I can recognize narrative patterns and dramatic techniques across plays

competencies.raw: Reflect on cultural contexts and diverse perspectives in drama
i_can_statements: I can analyze how cultural contexts shape dramatic works and interpretations
I can understand perspectives of diverse playwrights and storytellers
I can recognize representation and casting considerations in theatre
I can consider social and historical contexts of dramatic works

competencies.raw: Create written scripts and script documentation
i_can_statements: I can format scripts according to industry standards
I can write clear stage directions and technical specifications
I can document blocking and movement in scripts
I can maintain script versions and track revisions

competencies.raw: Communicate directorial vision to actors and creative teams
i_can_statements: I can articulate my directorial vision clearly and compellingly
I can give effective direction and feedback to actors
I can collaborate with designers and technical team members
I can communicate artistic vision while remaining open to input

competencies.raw: Document production choices and directorial decisions
i_can_statements: I can create prompt books and rehearsal notes
I can photograph or video-record staging and blocking
I can write production notes explaining directorial choices
I can document the development process and creative journey

competencies.raw: Present scripts and directorial work to audiences and stakeholders
i_can_statements: I can pitch scripts and productions to audiences and producers
I can give presentations explaining directorial concepts and choices
I can engage audiences in discussions about scripts and productions
I can advocate for artistic vision while considering audience reception

competencies.raw: Connect scripts to contemporary issues and personal experience
i_can_statements: I can identify how dramatic works address contemporary social issues
I can create scripts informed by personal experience and observation
I can adapt scripts to reflect current contexts and relevance
I can use theatre to explore meaningful themes and questions

competencies.raw: Explore diverse theatrical traditions and forms
i_can_statements: I can study and appreciate theatrical traditions from various cultures
I can understand different theatrical forms and genres
I can experiment with non-traditional dramatic forms and approaches
I can incorporate diverse theatrical styles into directorial work

competencies.raw: Engage with professional theatre and dramaturgy
i_can_statements: I can study professional productions and analyze directorial choices
I can research playwrights, directors, and theatrical movements
I can understand dramaturgical approaches to script selection and development
I can engage with professional theatre communities and practices

competencies.raw: Develop personal artistic voice as director and writer
i_can_statements: I can identify my artistic interests and directorial preferences
I can develop a distinctive voice as a writer and director
I can pursue artistic passions through script selection and creation
I can envision directing and writing as lifelong creative practices

areas.name: Script Analysis and Interpretation
topics: Identifying plot structure and dramatic arc
Historical and cultural contexts of plays

areas.name: Directorial Vision and Staging
topics: Developing directorial concepts and interpretive frameworks
Use of stage space and spatial relationships
Adapting scripts for different spaces and resources

areas.name: Script Writing and Development
topics: Adaptation of existing works for the stage

areas.name: Theatrical Forms and Traditions
topics: Comedy, tragedy, and other dramatic genres
